I have flat feet, so it may just be the shape of my foot. The rest of the shoe is comfortable. The heel is higher than my Chameleons, so would take a little getting used to. Still prefer Merrel’s to anything else.

I hiked the grand canyon in them.  I've worn the moab 2 shoes for around 6 months now and they are still very uncomfortable.  Specifically, the moab 2 hurts my right big toe.  Unfortunately, I've had to stop wearing them.  I wish I had returned them.  Here's hoping they bring back the original moab!

Walking, the heel feels like it is a bit short...like the sole raises up from the ground a little more forward than other shoes.  My only real complaint is that the shoes have developed a quiet creaky noise when walking. I don't notice it when outdoors or in noisy settings, but it is noticeable at work in a quiet office. I thought about returning them, but procrastinated too long so I'm stuck with it. So far it hasn't gotten worse.

title: They work great for casual walking
text: Husband has been wearing these for years. They are not the ultimate running shoe (but then look at price point). They work great for casual walking, hiking, everyday use. He likes them as they are lined. They are not waterproof by any means but they do keep moisture out to a point. Most running shoes are open mesh for reason...to let the heat out. These have a lining as they are designed for trail use. Good support and average padding. Key features for my husband really are grippy sole and lining which allows raining walks with dog and mowing wet grass without getting feet wet due to the lining.
